frameworks.name: context-dependent perceptual decision making
frameworks.one_line: decision processes flexibly adjust to context across distributed brain networks

frameworks.name: cognitive control
frameworks.one_line: prefrontal control framework classically invoked for flexible behavior

frameworks.name: distributed network computation
frameworks.one_line: flexibility implemented across parietal, sensory, motor, and subcortical regions

core_question: What neural mechanisms enable perceptual decisions to flexibly adjust to changing context?
key_assumptions: natural environments demand variable decision processes
context dependency is implemented via diverse neural computations
flexibility is not localized to prefrontal cortex alone
manipulations: review across studies varying context, rules, and behavioral demands
key_findings: context-dependent neural activity arises in a distributed network
posterior parietal, sensory, motor, and subcortical regions show context modulation
prefrontal cognitive-control areas are part of this distributed flexibility
interpretations: flexible decision making depends on a distributed network, not a single control hub
investigating this network is key to understanding decision flexibility
